description: Trèsind Studio is the most celebrated restaurant in Dubai and the only Indian restaurant in the world to hold three Michelin stars — a historic distinction earned in 2025 after just four years in the guide. Chef Himanshu Saini's immersive 24-seat tasting menu journeys through four regions of India — Thar Desert, Deccan Plateau, Coastal Plains, and the Northern Plains — with theatrical presentation and extraordinary technical precision. Ranked No. 27 on the World's 50 Best Restaurants list, this is a defining dining experience of the contemporary global scene. Located at St. Regis Gardens on Palm Jumeirah.
highlights: World's only three-Michelin-star Indian restaurant; No. 27 World's 50 Best; chef Himanshu Saini; immersive 24-seat tasting menu; Palm Jumeirah address; Art of Hospitality award winner.
restaurant_setting: Design-led dining room in luxury hotel complex
sources: Michelin Guide Dubai 2025; World's 50 Best Restaurants 2024; Elite Traveler; Condé Nast Traveler; The National

description: FZN is one of the most remarkable restaurant openings in recent global fine dining history — earning three Michelin stars within months of opening in November 2024 at Atlantis The Palm. Swedish chef Björn Frantzén, who also holds three stars in Stockholm and Singapore, delivers a nine-course tasting menu that fuses Nordic technique with Japanese precision in an intimate 27-seat restaurant spread across two floors, with an open kitchen as the centrepiece. The beverage programme spans over 1,200 wines and a non-alcoholic pairing developed over a decade. Ranked No. 22 on MENA's 50 Best Restaurants 2026.
highlights: Three Michelin stars within months of opening; Nordic-Japanese fine dining; 27-seat intimate counter; chef Björn Frantzén and executive chef Torsten Vildgaard (ex-Noma); over 1,200 wines.
sources: Michelin Guide Dubai 2025; Elite Traveler; The National; Vogue Scandinavia; Gault & Millau UAE

description: Il Ristorante – Niko Romito is Dubai's most refined Italian dining experience — a two-Michelin-star restaurant at the Bulgari Resort on Jumeirah Bay Island, where chef Niko Romito's philosophy of stripping Italian cuisine to its essential nature produces dishes of extraordinary purity and restraint. The setting is as dramatic as the food: a private island resort with panoramic sea views, a dramatic black-ceilinged dining room, and the exclusivity of a Bulgari address. Resident chef Giacomo Amicucci leads the kitchen, trained at the three-Michelin-star Reale in Italy.
highlights: Two Michelin stars; Bulgari Resort on private Jumeirah Bay Island; chef Niko Romito's philosophy of Italian purism; panoramic Arabian Gulf views; exclusive island setting.
restaurant_setting: Luxury hotel dining room on private island
sources: Michelin Guide Dubai 2025; Elite Traveler; The National; Condé Nast Traveler

description: STAY by Yannick Alléno is one of Dubai's most consistently celebrated fine dining addresses — a two-Michelin-star restaurant at the One&Only The Palm, set in a breathtaking colonial-style property at the end of the Palm with tropical gardens, a private dock, and vaulted ceilings hung with black crystal chandeliers. Chef Yannick Alléno's cuisine blends classical French precision with modern technique; the legendary 'pastry library' is one of Dubai's most talked-about dessert experiences. Retained two stars every year since the guide's inaugural 2022 edition.
highlights: Two Michelin stars retained since 2022; One&Only The Palm setting; colonial-style architecture with tropical gardens; Yannick Alléno's signature 'pastry library'; private island feel.
sources: Michelin Guide Dubai 2025; Elite Traveler; The National; Condé Nast Traveler; Time Out Dubai

description: Row on 45 is Dubai's most sophisticated sky-dining experience — a two-Michelin-star restaurant on the 45th floor of Grosvenor House in Dubai Marina, where chef Jason Atherton serves a 17-course tasting menu in a penthouse-like space for just 22 guests, with panoramic views of Palm Jumeirah and the Marina skyline. The menu unfolds across three acts and three separate spaces — from welcome snacks to a chef's library finale — blending fine Japanese ingredients with French technique in a format ranked 36th on MENA's 50 Best Restaurants 2026. The four-seat chef's table is one of Dubai's most coveted reservations.
highlights: Two Michelin stars; 45th-floor views of Palm Jumeirah and Dubai Marina; chef Jason Atherton; 17-course menu in 22 seats; three-act immersive format; MENA's 50 Best 2026.
sources: Michelin Guide Dubai 2025; MENA's 50 Best Restaurants 2026; Gault & Millau UAE; Condé Nast Traveler

description: Ossiano is one of Dubai's most theatrically iconic restaurants — a one-Michelin-star seafood and contemporary tasting menu experience at Atlantis The Palm, where diners descend a dramatic staircase into a room with floor-to-ceiling aquarium windows looking onto 65,000 fish and marine life. The multi-course menu is as ambitious as the setting, with an ever-evolving focus on premium seafood, and the restaurant is consistently cited by international media as one of Dubai's most unique dining experiences. The chandelier-lit, jazz-accompanied dining room is one of the city's most memorable.
highlights: One Michelin star; iconic Atlantis underwater aquarium setting; dramatic floor-to-ceiling marine tanks; premium seafood tasting menu; jazz pianist; consistently praised by global media.
sources: Michelin Guide Dubai 2025; Elite Traveler; Condé Nast Traveler; Time Out Dubai; The Luxury Editor

description: Dinner by Heston Blumenthal is one of Dubai's most intellectually engaging fine dining experiences — a one-Michelin-star restaurant at Atlantis The Royal that revisits historic British recipes from the 13th to the 19th century, reimagined through Blumenthal's extraordinary technical lens. The Dubai outpost occupies a spectacular position with sweeping views of the Palm Jumeirah from floor-to-ceiling windows, and the theatrics of the kitchen — particularly the iconic meat fruit and tipsy cake — make this a destination for diners who want spectacle alongside culinary substance.
highlights: One Michelin star; Heston Blumenthal's historic British menu reimagined; iconic meat fruit and tipsy cake; Atlantis The Royal setting; dramatic Palm Jumeirah views.
restaurant_setting: Luxury hotel dining room with sea views
sources: Michelin Guide Dubai 2025; Elite Traveler; Condé Nast Traveler; Time Out Dubai

description: Hakkasan Dubai is the UAE outpost of the globally acclaimed Cantonese fine dining brand — a one-Michelin-star restaurant in the Jumeirah Emirates Towers where executive chef Andy Toh applies traditional Cantonese techniques to contemporary results in a dramatically dark, lacquered dining room of legendary visual impact. One of the city's most consistently popular upscale Asian restaurants, Hakkasan has maintained its Michelin star across every edition of the Dubai guide and delivers one of the most reliable luxury experiences in the city for both business dinners and celebratory evenings.
highlights: One Michelin star retained since 2022; globally acclaimed Cantonese brand; dramatic lacquered interior; Emirates Towers address; executive chef Andy Toh; one of Dubai's most reliable luxury Asian addresses.
restaurant_setting: Design-led dining room in hotel tower
sources: Michelin Guide Dubai 2025; Elite Traveler; Harper's Bazaar Arabia; Time Out Dubai

description: Zuma Dubai is the city's most iconic Japanese dining institution and a benchmark of the Dubai smart set — a DIFC landmark spread across three floors of warm-hued wood and steel, with a focal-point bar specialising in sake and spectacular cocktails, a live robata grill, and a sushi counter. No. 19 on MENA's 50 Best Restaurants 2025, Zuma achieves the rare combination of authentic izakaya cuisine at fine-dining standard in an atmosphere that draws everyone from deal-makers to social dining enthusiasts. The Saturday brunch is one of Dubai's most coveted weekend events.
highlights: No. 19 MENA's 50 Best Restaurants 2025; DIFC landmark; three-floor contemporary Japanese setting; robata grill and sushi counter; legendary Saturday brunch; Michelin Guide selected.
sources: MENA's 50 Best Restaurants 2025; Michelin Guide Selected; Time Out Dubai; Condé Nast Traveler; 50 Best Discovery

description: La Dame de Pic Dubai is the Middle East outpost of celebrated French chef Anne-Sophie Pic's global dining empire — a one-Michelin-star restaurant in the Four Seasons DIFC that brings Pic's signature aromatic precision, unexpected flavour combinations, and impeccably presented French technique to a sleek, contemporary dining room with Burj Khalifa views. One of only a handful of Dubai restaurants representing classical French fine dining at a global standard, La Dame de Pic is consistently cited by Michelin inspectors for its distinctive culinary personality and flavour harmony.
highlights: One Michelin star; Anne-Sophie Pic's signature aromatic French cuisine; Four Seasons DIFC; Burj Khalifa views; one of Dubai's most distinctive fine dining addresses.
sources: Michelin Guide Dubai 2025; Elite Traveler; Condé Nast Traveler; The National

description: Orfali Bros Bistro is Dubai's most compelling chef-driven, personality-led restaurant — a one-Michelin-star destination where Syrian brothers Mohamad, Wassim, and Omar Orfali cook a tasting menu that blends Middle Eastern flavour memory with contemporary European technique, resulting in deeply personal and emotionally charged dishes. The most accessible and relatable of Dubai's starred restaurants in terms of price and atmosphere, Orfali Bros has garnered enormous international attention and represents the strongest argument for a home-grown Dubai dining identity. Mohamad Orfali dedicated his 2024 Michelin star to young Arab chefs.
highlights: One Michelin star; Syrian brothers Orfali; Middle Eastern-European tasting menu; most affordable starred experience; deeply personal concept; cult critical favourite.
sources: Michelin Guide Dubai 2025; The National; Condé Nast Traveler; Eater; Time Out Dubai

description: Hōseki is Dubai's most intimate and exclusive omakase experience — a one-Michelin-star Japanese restaurant at the Bulgari Resort with just nine counter seats (twelve in some configurations) and a single chef performing the entire service. Fish is flown in daily from Tsukiji market in Tokyo, and each guest receives undivided attention in an experience that mirrors the finest Tokyo sushi counters. One of the most distinctive dining experiences in the Middle East, Hōseki — 'gemstone' in Japanese — demands well in advance reservation planning and delivers accordingly.
highlights: One Michelin star; ultra-exclusive 9-seat omakase counter; daily Tsukiji fish; single-chef service; Bulgari Resort on private island; one of Dubai's most intimate dining experiences.
sources: Michelin Guide Dubai 2025; Elite Traveler; The Luxury Editor; Condé Nast Traveler

description: Al Muntaha is one of Dubai's most architecturally spectacular restaurants — a one-Michelin-star contemporary European dining room perched on the 27th floor of the Burj Al Arab, with vast curved windows offering 360-degree views of the Arabian Gulf and the Dubai coastline. The French-inspired menu focuses on premium seasonal ingredients, and the combination of culinary ambition and the world's most famous hotel setting makes Al Muntaha a genuinely compelling special-occasion destination for international visitors. The setting alone is worth the visit; the food earns the star.
highlights: One Michelin star; 27th floor of Burj Al Arab; 360-degree Arabian Gulf views; contemporary European cuisine; world's most iconic hotel setting.
restaurant_setting: Skyline dining room in iconic hotel
sources: Michelin Guide Dubai 2025; Elite Traveler; Condé Nast Traveler; Time Out Dubai

description: Smoked Room Dubai is the Middle East outpost of Dabiz Muñoz's acclaimed Madrid restaurant — a one-Michelin-star fire-omakase experience at the St. Regis Gardens on Palm Jumeirah, where just 14 guests gather around a single large semi-circular table overlooking an open kitchen of wood fires and grills. Chef Dani García's 11-course 'fire omakase' menu showcases extraordinary technique and creativity, with standout dishes including sea cucumber carbonara and masterfully crafted smoked desserts. The restaurant achieved its star within six months of opening, making it one of Dubai's most remarkable recent arrivals.
highlights: One Michelin star within six months of opening; Dani García's fire omakase concept; 14-seat semi-circular table; open-fire kitchen theatre; Palm Jumeirah address.
restaurant_setting: Intimate dining room with open fire kitchen
sources: Michelin Guide Dubai 2025; Sotheby's Realty AE; Elite Traveler; Condé Nast Traveler

description: 11 Woodfire is Dubai's most creative live-fire restaurant — a one-Michelin-star destination in Jumeirah where chef Akmal Anuar cooks an entirely wood-fired menu that builds a compelling case for fire as a sophisticated fine-dining technique rather than a casual one. The concept is rooted in the philosophy that extreme heat and smoke can achieve extraordinary complexity, and the restaurant has become a favourite among local diners and food media for its balance of technical ambition and warmth of atmosphere. One of the most distinctive and personally driven restaurants in the city.
highlights: One Michelin star; entirely wood-fired tasting menu; chef Akmal Anuar; one of Dubai's most original fine dining concepts; cult local favourite.
sources: Michelin Guide Dubai 2025; Condé Nast Traveler; Time Out Dubai; The National

description: Nobu Dubai is one of the city's most consistently popular and socially compelling restaurants — a Michelin Guide-selected Japanese-Peruvian destination relocated in 2023 to a striking position on the 22nd floor of Atlantis The Palm, with spectacular Arabian Gulf and Dubai Marina views from a slick, contemporary dining room. Nobu Matsuhisa's signature black cod miso, yellowtail sashimi with jalapeño, and the full omakase experience attract a star-studded, globally diverse clientele. One of the most reliably glamorous evenings in Dubai.
highlights: Michelin Guide selected; 22nd-floor Atlantis The Palm; Japanese-Peruvian fusion; legendary black cod miso; celebrity clientele; spectacular sea views.
restaurant_setting: High-rise dining room with sea views
sources: Michelin Guide Selected; Time Out Dubai; Condé Nast Traveler; Harper's Bazaar Arabia; Bloomberg

description: La Petite Maison Dubai — universally known as LPM — is one of the most institutionalised and beloved restaurants in the city, a DIFC fixture since 2012 that functions as the social dining room of Dubai's business and media elite. The Niçoise menu of shared plates — salt-baked sea bass, marinated lamb cutlets, pasta, and côte d'Azur vegetables — is served in an elegant bougainvillea-draped setting with an all-consuming Provençal atmosphere. Described by Gault & Millau UAE as 'not so much a restaurant as an institution', LPM is the definitive non-Michelin luxury address in Dubai.
highlights: DIFC institution since 2012; beloved of Dubai's media and business elite; Niçoise menu of shared plates; bougainvillea-draped terrace; Michelin Guide selected; Gault & Millau recognition.
sources: Michelin Guide Selected; Gault & Millau UAE; Time Out Dubai; The National; Condé Nast Traveler; Financial Times

description: Coya Dubai is the city's defining Peruvian-Latin American dining destination — a flagship restaurant at the Four Seasons Resort Dubai at Jumeirah Beach with a dramatically designed interior of Incan-inspired handmade furniture, live music, and an open kitchen that creates one of the most kinetic and socially compelling atmospheres in the city. Following its success at the original Mayfair location, Coya Dubai expanded the concept to a grander scale that has made it an anchor of Dubai's luxury social calendar, particularly for its celebrated Saturday brunch. Michelin Guide selected.
highlights: Michelin Guide selected; Four Seasons Jumeirah Beach; dramatic Incan-inspired interior; live music and open kitchen; famous Saturday brunch; leading Peruvian-Latin dining in the city.
restaurant_setting: Design-led dining room in luxury hotel
sources: Michelin Guide Selected; Time Out Dubai; Condé Nast Traveler; Harper's Bazaar Arabia; The National
hotel_name_hint: Four Seasons Resort Dubai at Jumeirah Beach

description: Amazonico Dubai is the city's most theatrical Latin American dining and social experience — a DIFC landmark designed as an immersive artificial jungle, with multiple levels including the main dining room, a bar, lounge, and the Paraíso rooftop club, drawing Dubai's most fashion-conscious clientele to a setting of extraordinary visual impact. Resident DJs and percussionists perform every night, and the Brazilian and pan-Latin menu — from wood-grilled meats to extraordinary cocktails — delivers consistently high quality beneath the spectacle. Michelin Guide selected.
highlights: Michelin Guide selected; DIFC jungle-themed multi-level venue; Brazilian and Latin American menu; Paraíso rooftop club; resident DJs and percussionists; leading social dining destination.
sources: Michelin Guide Selected; Harper's Bazaar Arabia; Time Out Dubai; Condé Nast Traveler

description: Moonrise is one of Dubai's most acclaimed chef-driven tasting menu restaurants — a one-Michelin-star destination that has established itself as a leading address for inventive, produce-led contemporary cuisine in a city dominated by global hotel brands. The restaurant's intimate format, thoughtful beverage programme, and commitment to creative, seasonal cooking make it a cult favourite among Dubai's serious dining community and a compelling alternative for visitors seeking something beyond the celebrity-chef hotel circuit.
highlights: One Michelin star; independent chef-driven concept; inventive contemporary tasting menu; intimate format; cult favourite among Dubai food insiders.
sources: Michelin Guide Dubai 2025; Time Out Dubai; The National; Condé Nast Traveler

description: Tasca by José Avillez is the Dubai outpost of Portugal's most celebrated contemporary chef — a one-Michelin-star restaurant at the W Dubai – The Palm that brings Avillez's signature Portuguese-influenced contemporary European cuisine to a glamorous setting overlooking the Palm Jumeirah. One of very few Portuguese fine dining concepts in the Middle East, Tasca combines a relaxed but polished atmosphere with exceptional technique, and the fish and seafood preparations in particular reflect the chef's deep roots in Portuguese culinary tradition.
highlights: One Michelin star; José Avillez's signature Portuguese-European cuisine; W Dubai The Palm; exceptional fish and seafood; rare high-quality Portuguese fine dining in the Middle East.
sources: Michelin Guide Dubai 2025; Elite Traveler; Condé Nast Traveler; The National

description: At.mosphere is the world's highest restaurant and bar — a Michelin Guide-selected dining room on the 122nd floor of the Burj Khalifa, offering one of the most dramatic dining settings on earth with 360-degree views of Dubai, the desert, and the Arabian Gulf that stretch to the horizon. The European fine dining menu is ambitious and well-executed, and the combination of an exceptional wine programme and the psychological weight of dining above the clouds makes At.mosphere a genuinely singular experience that no luxury visitor to Dubai should overlook.
highlights: World's highest restaurant at 122nd floor of Burj Khalifa; Michelin Guide selected; 360-degree views of Dubai; European fine dining menu; strong wine programme; one of the world's most extraordinary dining settings.
restaurant_setting: Skyline dining room in iconic tower
sources: Michelin Guide Selected; Elite Traveler; Condé Nast Traveler; Time Out Dubai

description: Avatāra is one of the most internationally significant restaurant concepts in Dubai — a one-Michelin-star entirely vegetarian Indian tasting menu where chef Rahul Rana demonstrates that vegetarian fine dining can achieve the same depth of complexity, technique, and luxury as any meat-centred menu. The restaurant challenges assumptions about Indian cuisine and about vegetarian dining simultaneously, serving an 18-course experience that draws on Ayurvedic principles and centuries of subcontinental culinary tradition. A landmark of progressive Indian gastronomy.
highlights: One Michelin star; entirely vegetarian Indian tasting menu; chef Rahul Rana; 18-course Ayurvedic-inspired menu; challenges assumptions about both vegetarian and Indian fine dining.
sources: Michelin Guide Dubai 2025; Elite Traveler; Condé Nast Traveler; The National

description: Pierchic is Dubai's most romantically positioned restaurant — a Michelin Guide-selected Italian seafood destination set at the end of a private pier extending into the Arabian Gulf at Al Qasr, Madinat Jumeirah, with unobstructed views of the Burj Al Arab and the open sea. One of the city's most enduring special-occasion restaurants, Pierchic combines premium fresh seafood, handmade pasta, and Italian culinary precision with a setting of exceptional natural drama that no design-led interior can replicate. The walk along the pier at sunset is an experience in itself.
highlights: Michelin Guide selected; pier setting over Arabian Gulf; views of Burj Al Arab; Italian seafood and pasta; Madinat Jumeirah; Dubai's most romantic dining setting.
sources: Michelin Guide Selected; Time Out Dubai; Condé Nast Traveler; Harper's Bazaar Arabia; DAMAC Properties

description: Jamavar Dubai is the newest star in Dubai's acclaimed Indian fine dining constellation — awarded its first Michelin star in 2025 on its debut in the guide, located in the Opera district and part of the Leela Hotels group that also holds a Michelin star in London. The restaurant offers perfectly balanced, ingredient-led North Indian cooking across a menu that moves through the breadth of the subcontinent's regional traditions, with prime ingredients elevated by classical technique and presented in a dining room of understated elegance. A compelling counterpoint to the theatrics of Trèsind Studio.
highlights: One Michelin star 2025 debut; Leela group (also holds London star); Opera District address; North Indian regional cooking with prime ingredients; elegant understated setting.
sources: Michelin Guide Dubai 2025; The National; Elite Traveler; Khaleej Times
